It goes all the way back to Buckminster Fuller who said you can't fix an existing system. You have to build a new system at the edge and let that become the new gravity center. John Hagel and John Seeley Brown identified this as disruptive things happen at the edge. The poster child here is Nestle created Nespresso in 1976.
